text: Some think verses 3-6 refer to the past history of God’s people in the time of the Judges; some, that they look forward to the future of the ten tribes which revolted against Juda. Verse 3 in the original begins simply: ‘Long must Israel wait’.

vv. 16-18, cf. III Kg. 15.13-16. The statement there made, that Asa and Baasa were at war all through their reign, perhaps means only that their relations were unfriendly, not (in view of the present passage) that there was any border fighting.
